---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

<div style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 2.5rem; padding: 3rem 0 2rem 0; flex-wrap: wrap;">

<img src="assets/profile.jpg" style="width: 180px; height: 180px; object-fit: cover; border-radius: 50%; border: 3px solid #dee2e6; flex-shrink: 0;">

<div style="flex: 1; min-width: 260px;">

# Zheng He

**Doctoral Candidate (Dr. med.)**

🏛 [Starkuvienė Lab](https://www.bioquant.uni-heidelberg.de/groups/starkuviene), BioQuant, Heidelberg University · Heidelberg<br>
🏥 Medizinische Fakultät Mannheim, Heidelberg University · Mannheim

*Supervised by Prof. Dr. med. Michael Keese & Prof. Dr. Vytautė Starkuvienė-Erfle*<br>
*Doktorvater: PD Dr. med. Martin Sigl*

<div style="margin-top: 1rem; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem;">
<a href="mailto:zheng.he@bioquant.uni-heidelberg.de" class="btn btn-primary btn-sm">✉ zheng.he@bioquant.uni-heidelberg.de</a>
<a href="mailto:hezheng.surgeon@gmail.com" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm">✉ hezheng.surgeon@gmail.com</a>
<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=oPz_JBcAAAAJ" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">🎓 Google Scholar</a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-0374-0368" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">ORCID</a>
<a href="https://github.com/zhenghe-research" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" target="_blank">GitHub</a>
</div>

</div>
</div>

---

My research focuses on the **phenotypic switch of vascular smooth muscle cells (VSMCs)** — the process by which VSMCs switch between contractile and synthetic states in vascular disease. I combine high-throughput bioinformatics analysis with wet lab validation to identify small molecule modulators of this transition, with potential implications for atherosclerosis and in-stent restenosis.

I am also involved in research on **ABCB5⁺ mesenchymal stem cells** and their regenerative potential.

---

## Research Interests

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Vascular Biology**
VSMC phenotypic plasticity, contractile-to-synthetic transition, in-stent restenosis, atherosclerosis
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Bioinformatics**
Bulk RNA-seq, scRNA-seq, Mendelian randomization, gene signature discovery, transcription factor analysis
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
**Translational Research**
Small molecule drug candidates (Resveratrol, Galangin), stem cell differentiation, ABCB5⁺ MSC biology
:::
::::

---

## News

- **Apr 2026** — Doctoral thesis submitted for review (Universität Heidelberg)
- **2025** — Review paper published: *The Differentiation and Regeneration Potential of ABCB5⁺ Mesenchymal Stem Cells* · *J Clin Med*
- **2021** — Paper published in *Disease Markers* on bioinformatics analysis of venous thromboembolism
